title: Rank-averaged model-diversity ensemble
summary: The strongest snippets appear feature-focused, likely with one dominant GBDT setup.
rationale: The strongest snippets appear feature-focused, likely with one dominant GBDT setup. Public tabular practice supports mixing LightGBM, CatBoost, and XGBoost because they handle categorical interactions and splits differently; CatBoost is especially useful for high-cardinality categoricals such as Driver and Driver_Race.
implementation_hint: Train several leakage-safe OOF models over the same engineered table: LightGBM with categorical features, CatBoost with native categories/ordered boosting, XGBoost or HistGradientBoosting on encoded features, and a simple hazard-prior-only model. Average OOF/test predictions as ranks or logits with weights tuned on grouped OOF AUC. Use repeated seeds and keep categorical/target encodings fold-local.
expected_effect: Could add small but valuable AUC gains through complementary errors, likely more robust than adding more arithmetic features to an already saturated feature set.
risk: Random CV weight tuning can overfit; grouped Race/Year OOF is needed. Calibration alone will not improve AUC for a single model if monotonic, so the benefit must come from ensemble diversity and rank blending.
